english: to hear, to listen eagerly; to be willing, su żig ₒdúg-par dgá-na (su zhig 'dug par dga' na) if anybody will stay here voluntarily Dzl.; to have a mind, to intend, to wish, kyod ráb-tu byuṅ-bar dgá-am (khyod rab tu 'byung bar dga' am) do you intend to take orders? Dzl.; bdág-gis ras ₒdi . . . sbyín-par dgao (bdag gis ras 'di . . . sbyin par dga'o) I should like to present this cloth to . . . Dzl.; méd-par byá-bar dgá-na (med par bya bar dga' na) as I wish to annihilate . . . Dzl.; gar dgá-bar (gar dga' bar) (or gaṅ dgá-ba der (gang dga' ba der)) soṅ go (song go) whereever you like Dzl.; seldom with the accus.: ₒdzóm-pa de dga-sté ('dzom pa de dga' ste) as you now enjoy an abundance Mil.; with the instr. case: des dgá-bar śóg-cig (des dga' bar shog cig), may you be cheered, comforted by it Dzl.; frq. absolutely: dgá-bar byéd-pa (dga' bar byed pa) to make glad, to rejoice, C. also: to caress, to fondle. -- II. sbst. joy, dgá-bai rtam byéd-pa (dga' ba'i rtam byed pa) to express one's joy Dzl.; dgá-bai sems (dga' ba'i sems) id.; dé-la ráb-tu dgá-bai sems skyés-so (de la rab tu dga' ba'i sems skyes so) he found great delight in it Dzl.; compounds v. below. -- III. adj. 1. glad, pleased, enjoying, ṅa dgá-ba ma yín-pas (nga dga' ba ma yin pas) as I was not pleased with it Dzl.; de-la mi dgá-ba (de la mi dga' ba), W. *mi gá-kan* (*mi dga' mkhan*), not favourably disposed towards, unfriendly, inimical to; dgá-bar byéd-pa (dga' bar byed pa) to make glad, to delight, bu čuṅ dgá-ba byéd-pai yo-byád (bu chung dga' ba byed pa'i yo byad) things which delight little children, play-things Glr. -- Less frq. 2. charming, sweet, pleasing, agreeable, beautiful, lhág-par dgá-ru ₒgro (lhag par dga' ru 'gro) she is getting more and more beautiful; C. in a general sense: good, cf. below: dga-bdé (dga' bde). -- 3. as a proper name = नन्द Tar. Comp. and deriv. dga-grágs (dga' grags) ₒúr-ba ('ur ba) to give cheers, to raise shouts of joy Mil. -- dga-grógs (dga' grogs) a participator of joy, gen. with reference to husband or wife (col. *ga-róg* (*ga rog*)). -- dga-mgú (dga' mgu) great joy, dga-mgú-ba (dga' mgu ba), dga-mgu-ráṅ-ba (dga' mgu rang ba) to have great pleasure, to be very glad, to be delighted, frq., dgá-żiṅ mgú-la yi-ráṅs-pár ₒgyúr-ba (dga' zhing mgu la yi rangs par 'gyur ba) id. Glr. frq.; yet dga-mgúr spyód-pa (dga' mgur spyod pa) to indulge in sensual pleasure Pth., Stg., bu-mo daṅ (bu mo dang) with a girl. -- dga-stón (dga' ston) feast, public festivity; dga-stón-gyi gdán-sa (dga' ston gyi gdan sa) the place of a feast Glr.; bsú-ba dgá-ba (bsu ba dga' ba) festivities of welcome Glr.; dga-stón byéd-pa (dga' ston byed pa) to celebrate a festival; ₒgyéd-pa ('gyed pa) to spread a feast, to distribute festival dishes; fig. rná-bai dga-stón (rna ba'i dga' ston) a feast or treat to the ears Glr. -- dga-bdé (dga' bde) 1. joy, lus sems dga-bdés kyáb-par ₒgyur (lus sems dga' bdes khyab par 'gyur) Glr. 2. (Ts. col. *gaṅ-dé* (*gang de*)) good, = yág-po (yag po), (of servants, dogs etc.) C.; *mí-la ga-dé jhé-pa* (*mi la dga' bde byed pa*) to treat a person kindly, with affection C. -- dga-ₒdún (dga' 'dun) wedding, nuptial festivities Sch. (seems to be a word not generally known). -- dga-ₒdód (dga' 'dod) n. of the plain of Lhasa, or at least of the northern part of it. -- dga-ldán (dga' ldan) joyful 1. n. of a residence of gods, or of one of the heavens, Ssk. तुषित v. Köpp. i. 265. 2. n. of one of the great monasteries near Lhasa, founded by Tsongkhapa, about the year 1407, v. Köpp. II, 345. 3. gzím-sa dga-ldan (gzim sa dga' ldan) n. of the royal castle of residence at Lhasa; dga-ldán-pa (dga' ldan pa) n. of a sect = dge-lúgs-pa (dge lugs pa). -- dgá-bo (dga' bo) = dga-bdé (dga' bde) 2. good C. -- dga-sdug-drag-żan (dga' sdug drag zhan) good and bad, strong and weak, of articles of merchandise and the like C. -- dga-spró (dga' spro) joy, dga-spró dpag-tu-méd-pa tób-pa yin (dga' spro dpag tu med pa thob pa yin) he entered into a state of indescribable joy Mil. -- dgá-ma (dga' ma) n. of the goddess of joy Cs. -- dga-ma-ₒdúr (dga' ma 'dur) C., W. (col. *gá-man-dúr* (*dga' man 'dur*)) the trembling with joy, the state of being enraptured, in ecstasy. -- dgá-mo (dga' mo) 1. delightful, pleasing, charming, of news, of a speech W., of a landscape Mil. 2. delighted, joyous, cheerful W., *sem gá-mo rag* (*sems dga' mo rag*) I am cheerful; *gá-mo-čan* (*dga' mo can*) W. id.; *gá-mo jhé-pa* (*dga' mo byed pa*) C. to caress, to fondle. 3. pure, holy Sch., Dzl., prob. also Mil.; čós-pa dgá-mo (chos pa dga' mo) a godly priest. -- dga-tsór (dga' tshor) joy, *k̄o ga-tsór máṅ-po jhe* (*kho dga' tshor mang po byed*) C. he is very joyful; dga-tsór čé-ba (dga' tshor che ba) gratifying, delightful Mil. -- dga-ráṅs (dga' rangs) being glad, rejoicing, *dhé-la ga-ráṅ dhág-te* (*de la dga' rang dhag te*) C. being greatly delighted with it, -- dga-rís (dga' ris) v. ga-ri (ga ri), = gá-ża (ga zha).
= dgá-bar (dga' bar), raṅ-dgár (rang dgar) at pleasure, ad libitum, frq.: ci dgar (ci dgar) Pth. seems to mean: why.

I. to separate, confine, fold up (men, cattle, goods), dgar-byá (dgar bya) pyugs (phyugs) cattle to be penned in a fold Cs. rnas-nas dyár-ba (rnas nas dyar ba) to banish, to exile; dgár-bai dón-du (dgar ba'i don du) in a special sense, in particu-
